findings.note: unsafe pickle.loads on untrusted webhook body (RCE) and the signature header is never actually verified
query is parameterized (fine) but password is compared/stored in plaintext, no hashing
unsanitized zip_file.filename used directly in save path, allowing path traversal on write
unsanitized file.filename used in save path (path traversal); debug=True also left on
classic SQL injection: username interpolated directly into query via f-string
JWT header/payload base64-decoded but signature is never verified (accepts unsigned/forged tokens)
broken parsing plus signature check never completed; alg is trusted from the token's own header
json.loads on cookie has no signature/integrity check, so session fields are forgeable (though at least avoids pickle)
confusing/buggy param construction but value is still bound via placeholder, not concatenated into SQL
zipfile.extractall() with no member/path validation permits zip-slip write outside target dir
SSRF: arbitrary user-supplied URL fetched with no allowlist or timeout
SSRF: arbitrary user-supplied URL fetched with no allowlist or timeout
hash_password() result is discarded — plaintext password is returned/stored as the 'password_hash' field
user search string concatenated directly into SQL text via f-string (SQL injection)
fully parameterized query, no injection
parameterized query is safe; unrelated bug calling .lower without parens
open redirect: 'next' URL taken from query string and used unchecked in redirect()
jwt.decode called with no key or algorithm allowlist, so signature verification isn't meaningfully enforced
unsanitized filename enables path traversal on save; debug=True left on in production
parameterized query fine, but password is stored/compared in plaintext
parameter binding used correctly; SQL syntax typo is a functional bug, not a vuln
fully parameterized query, no injection
hardcoded signing secret ('secret_key') embedded in source for session integrity check
